title: hieracium caesium subsp. zabelianum
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. zabelianum, also known as Zabel's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and reduce inflammation due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. zabelianum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
